title: Fix CORS simulator incorrectly forcing preflight for credentialed simple requests
description: cors-simulator.ts:208 — `const isSimple = isSimpleMethod && hasOnlySimpleHeaders && !config.credentials` treats credentials as a preflight trigger. Per the Fetch spec, credentials do NOT trigger a preflight. A GET with cookies is a simple request. Line 218-219 also teaches the wrong rule to users. Fix: remove `&& !config.credentials` from isSimple and move credentials validation to response check only.
acceptanceCriteria: A credentialed GET/HEAD/POST with only simple headers does NOT trigger preflight
Credentials validation happens on the actual response check, not preflight decision
User-facing description no longer says 'Request includes credentials' as a preflight reason

title: Fix TLS 1.2 simulation using wrong message types for ServerKeyExchange, ServerHelloDone, ClientKeyExchange
description: tls-handshake.ts:443,460,475 — TLSMessageType lacks TLS 1.2-specific types. Engine reuses 'CertificateVerify' for ServerKeyExchange and 'Finished' for ServerHelloDone/ClientKeyExchange. The sequence diagram and step list show misleading labels. Fix: extend TLSMessageType to include 'ServerKeyExchange' | 'ServerHelloDone' | 'ClientKeyExchange' | 'ChangeCipherSpec' and use correctly.
acceptanceCriteria: TLS 1.2 handshake labels show correct message type names
TLSMessageType union includes all TLS 1.2 message types
Sequence diagram labels match actual TLS 1.2 spec message names

title: Fix ConnectionPoolVisualization nested setState causing stale data and double render
description: ConnectionPoolVisualization.tsx:142-264 — setQueue is called inside setConnections callback. The connection mutations from lines 151-240 are computed but discarded (line 264 returns prevConns unchanged). A second setConnections at line 268 re-processes independently with different random values. Fix: use useReducer with a single dispatch that atomically updates connections, queue, and metrics.
acceptanceCriteria: Connections, queue, and metrics are updated atomically in a single state transition
No double-render per tick
Pool exhaustion warning accurately reflects current state

title: Add Step Back and Pause buttons to playback controls
description: NetworkingModule.tsx:1939-1963 — Only Step (forward), Play All, and Reset exist. No way to go back one step (must reset and re-step). No way to pause during autoplay. Play button stays labeled 'Play All' during animation with no visual indicator. Fix: add Previous Step button (decrement stepIndex), toggle Play/Pause during autoplay, show pulsing indicator during play.
acceptanceCriteria: Previous Step button decrements stepIndex by 1, disabled at step 0
Play button toggles to Pause during autoplay
Visual indicator (e.g., pulsing dot or 'Playing...' text) shown during autoplay
Pause stops at current step without resetting

title: Replace 50+ hardcoded hex colors with CSS custom properties throughout module
description: NetworkingModule.tsx:215-332 (SequenceDiagram SVG uses #1e293b, #334155, #e2e8f0, #3b82f6, #60a5fa, #9ca3af, #6b7280), tls13-handshake.ts:442,450,457-458, dns-resolution.ts:167-168, cdn-flow.ts:199-217, serialization-comparison.ts:269-312, ConnectionPoolVisualization.tsx:423-478 — 50+ instances. SVG elements use inline hex; engine files return raw hex. Fix: use var(--primary), var(--border), etc. in SVG; move color mapping from engines to component layer.
acceptanceCriteria: Zero hardcoded hex values in SequenceDiagram SVG
Engine files return semantic identifiers not hex colors
Module renders correctly in both dark theme and (future) light theme
ConnectionPoolVisualization uses CSS variables for all colors

title: Increase all sub-11px text to minimum 11px across module
description: NetworkingModule.tsx — 34+ instances of text-[9px] (lines 735,740,755,763,1078,1082,1197,1220,1450,1498 etc.) and SVG fontSize='8' at lines 322,333. 9px text fails WCAG readability. Fix: bump text-[9px] to text-[11px] or text-xs (12px), SVG fontSize='8' to at least fontSize='10'.
acceptanceCriteria: No text element in the module renders below 11px
SVG state labels use fontSize 10 or higher
Layout remains compact without visual overflow

title: Add ARIA roles, labels, and live regions for screen reader support
description: NetworkingModule.tsx:158 — SVG has no role='img' or aria-label. Line 1922 — sidebar buttons lack aria-current. Lines 1960,2326 — step counter and description have no aria-live. Line 2218 — error message not linked via aria-describedby. ConnectionPoolVisualization.tsx:429 — color-only state distinction. Fix: add role='img' + aria-label to SVG, aria-current to active sidebar button, aria-live='polite' to dynamic content, aria-describedby to error.
acceptanceCriteria: SequenceDiagram SVG has role='img' and descriptive aria-label
Active sidebar protocol button has aria-current='true'
Step counter span has aria-live='polite'
Step description container has aria-live='polite'
Serialization error linked to textarea via aria-describedby
Connection states have text/icon backup beyond color

title: Add keyboard shortcuts for step navigation (arrow keys, space, R)
description: NetworkingModule.tsx — Zero keyboard event handlers. DataStructuresModule has onKeyDown at its line 2577. Fix: add Right arrow = step forward, Left arrow = step back, Space = play/pause, R = reset. Register on the module container element. Cross-module consistency with DataStructures.
acceptanceCriteria: Right arrow advances one step
Left arrow goes back one step
Space toggles play/pause
R resets to step 0
Shortcuts only active when module has focus

title: Add reduced-motion support to all animations
description: No file references prefers-reduced-motion. PacketJourneySimulator.tsx:199 (whileTap), 294 (pulsing dot); ConnectionPoolVisualization.tsx:333 (blinking warning), 432 (pulsing dots) all animate unconditionally. NetworkingModule.tsx bar width transitions also lack motion preference. Fix: wrap motion values with useReducedMotion() from framer-motion, provide static alternatives.
acceptanceCriteria: All animations respect prefers-reduced-motion: reduce
Pulsing dots replaced with static indicators when reduced motion preferred
Bar width transitions use instant transitions when motion reduced
Functionality unchanged with motion enabled

title: Make SequenceDiagram and HTTP grid responsive for mobile viewports
description: NetworkingModule.tsx:149 — SVG colWidth=200, DNS 5 columns = 1000px on 375px screen (illegible). Line 714 — grid-cols-3 for HTTP comparison does not collapse. Fix: make colWidth responsive based on container width (use ResizeObserver or useCallback ref), change grid-cols-3 to grid-cols-1 md:grid-cols-3.
acceptanceCriteria: DNS sequence diagram readable at 375px viewport width
HTTP comparison stacks vertically on mobile
SVG colWidth adapts to container or enables horizontal scroll
All text remains readable at mobile breakpoints

title: Split NetworkingModule.tsx (2814 lines) into per-visualization components
description: NetworkingModule.tsx contains 9 visualizer components, sidebar, properties panel, bottom panel, hook, and helpers in one file. Fix: extract each visualization into networking/ directory (e.g., TCPVisualization.tsx, CORSVisualization.tsx), hook into useNetworkingModule.ts, types into networking-types.ts, helpers into networking-helpers.ts.
acceptanceCriteria: NetworkingModule.tsx under 500 lines
Each visualization component in its own file
useNetworkingModule hook in separate file
All imports resolve correctly
No functional regression

title: Write tests for WebSocket, CORS, and TLS 1.2 engines (currently zero coverage)
description: websocket-lifecycle.ts, cors-simulator.ts, tls-handshake.ts have ZERO tests. CORS engine has a confirmed bug (NET-016). Fix: create test files covering connect/sendMessage/heartbeat/close for WS, simple/preflight/credentials/wildcard for CORS, TLS 1.2/1.3/0-RTT for TLS.
acceptanceCriteria: websocket-lifecycle.test.ts covers all 4 lifecycle phases + error states
cors-simulator.test.ts covers simple request, preflight, credentials, wildcard+creds, same-origin
tls-handshake.test.ts covers performHandshake, performResumption, performTLS12Handshake
All new tests pass

title: Fix TCP packet loss ACK calculation for pure-ACK segments
description: tcp-state-machine.ts:596 — `expectedAck = original.segment.seqNum + (original.segment.data?.length ?? 1)`. The ?? 1 fallback applies to pure ACKs that consume 0 seq numbers. Fix: compute as `(original.segment.data?.length ?? 0) + (original.segment.syn ? 1 : 0) + (original.segment.fin ? 1 : 0)`.
acceptanceCriteria: simulatePacketLoss on a pure ACK uses 0 for consumed bytes
SYN and FIN segments still correctly add 1
Data segments use actual data length

title: Fix CDN cache-purge flow skipping Origin Shield in response path
description: cdn-flow.ts:633 — Step 8 of cache-purge shows from:'Origin' to:'Edge POP' directly, skipping the Origin Shield cache step that should exist in the response path. Fix: split into two steps: Origin → Origin Shield (cache-store), then Origin Shield → Edge POP (cache-store).
acceptanceCriteria: Cache purge response path shows Origin → Shield → Edge
Both Shield and Edge cache-store steps have appropriate headers
Sequence diagram renders correctly with the additional step

title: Add disclosure that serialization binary sizes are simulated approximations
description: serialization-comparison.ts:85-91 — Protobuf/MessagePack/Avro sizes are fixed ratios of JSON (0.42/0.72/0.55). Hex dump at line 176 is fake. No user-facing disclosure. Fix: add prominent note in descriptions and UI: 'Sizes estimated from typical compression ratios, not actual encoding.' Consider adding a footnote in the visualization.
acceptanceCriteria: Description text for each binary format mentions sizes are approximate
Hex dump section includes note that output is simulated
User can distinguish simulated from actual values

title: Fix Play interval stale closure when data changes mid-animation
description: NetworkingModule.tsx:2614-2628 — handlePlay's setInterval captures messages.length from closure. If user changes sub-options while playing (e.g., toggle 0-RTT), the old interval uses stale length. handleSelect clears the timer but sub-option changes don't. Fix: use a ref for messages.length inside the interval, or add an effect that clears the timer when messages change.
acceptanceCriteria: Changing TLS toggle/DNS scenario/CDN scenario during autoplay does not cause index overshoot
Animation stops or restarts cleanly when data source changes

title: Add placeholder text to CORS config inputs and serialization textarea
description: NetworkingModule.tsx:2131-2216 — 5 inputs lack placeholder text (origin, target, method, headers, JSON textarea). Fix: add e.g. placeholder='https://app.example.com', 'e.g. PUT, DELETE', 'Content-Type, Authorization', etc.
acceptanceCriteria: All 5 inputs show helpful placeholder text when empty
Placeholders use realistic example values

title: Add error boundary for lazy-loaded Learn tab components
description: NetworkingModule.tsx:2316-2321 — PacketJourneySimulator and ConnectionPoolVisualization lazy-loaded with Suspense but no ErrorBoundary. Chunk load failure crashes the panel. Fix: wrap Suspense in ErrorBoundary with retry button.
acceptanceCriteria: Failed chunk load shows error message with retry button
Successful retry loads the components
Error does not propagate to parent module

title: Cap serialization textarea input and add size validation
description: NetworkingModule.tsx:2211 — textarea has no maxLength. User can paste arbitrarily large JSON causing browser freeze during JSON.parse + TextEncoder.encode. Fix: add maxLength=10000, validate size before parsing.
acceptanceCriteria: Textarea has maxLength=10000
Attempting to paste beyond limit is truncated
Performance remains smooth for max-length input

title: Eliminate duplicate data generation between hook and visualizer components
description: NetworkingModule.tsx:2402-2412 creates wsEvents in hook, while WebSocketVisualization:853-863 independently creates identical events. Same for DNS (hook:2390 vs DNSVisualization:568) and TLS. Fix: compute data once in hook and pass as props, or lift to shared context.
acceptanceCriteria: Each engine simulation runs exactly once per configuration
Visualizer components receive data as props
No duplicate useMemo computations for same data

title: Lazy-load engine files per protocol and defer unused data computation
description: NetworkingModule.tsx:17-48 — all 9 engine files imported eagerly. Lines 2374-2448 compute data for all protocols on mount. Fix: dynamic-import engines per active protocol, wrap data computation in conditional blocks keyed on active protocol.
acceptanceCriteria: Only the active protocol's engine is imported
Inactive protocol data is not computed
Switching protocols loads engine dynamically without perceptible delay

title: Add pre-training glossary tooltip for protocol-specific terms
description: Mayer's Pre-training principle scored 2/5. Users dropped into TCP with no explanation of SYN/ACK/FIN terms. Fix: add hover tooltips on key terms in descriptions (SYN, ACK, FIN, ISN, CNAME, TTL, etc.) and/or a 'Key Terms' collapsible section in properties panel per protocol.
acceptanceCriteria: At least 10 key networking terms have hover tooltips
Tooltips show brief definition + RFC reference
Tooltips accessible via keyboard (focusable)

title: Add completion feedback and progress tracking per protocol
description: Octalysis ACCOMPLISHMENT scored 2/5. No completion state, achievements, or 'you learned X' feedback. Fix: show completion animation when all steps viewed, track which protocols have been completed, display progress in sidebar (e.g., checkmark on completed protocols).
acceptanceCriteria: Completion animation plays when user reaches final step
Sidebar shows checkmark on protocols where all steps have been viewed
Progress persists during session (resets on page reload is acceptable)

title: Remove duplicate useEffect timer cleanup
description: NetworkingModule.tsx:2368-2372 and 2685-2689 — two identical useEffect blocks that cleanup timerRef.current on unmount. The second is dead code. Fix: remove the duplicate at lines 2685-2689.
acceptanceCriteria: Only one useEffect cleans up the timer on unmount
No functional regression

title: Remove 3 unused type imports
description: NetworkingModule.tsx:44 CDNScenarioDef, :46 APIComparisonResult, :48 SerializationResult — imported but never used. Fix: remove all three unused type imports.
acceptanceCriteria: No unused imports remain
TypeScript compilation passes

title: Fix DNS CNAME depth off-by-one (allows 9 hops instead of intended 8)
description: dns-resolution.ts:328 — `if (cnameDepth > MAX_CNAME_DEPTH)` with MAX_CNAME_DEPTH=8 allows depths 0-8 (9 hops). Fix: change to >= MAX_CNAME_DEPTH.
acceptanceCriteria: CNAME chain resolves up to exactly MAX_CNAME_DEPTH hops
Depth 8 triggers the abort message

title: Add time-travel scrubber for all protocol simulations
description: Innovation idea #4 (3/3 gates). Transform passive stepping into active exploration. Add a scrubber/slider that lets users drag to any step instantly. Like Redux DevTools time-travel debugging. Clicking the slider updates both the visualization and the description panel.
acceptanceCriteria: Horizontal slider appears below the canvas for all protocols
Dragging the slider instantly jumps to the corresponding step
Step counter updates to match slider position
Works with all 9 protocol visualizations

title: Add Protocol Decision Tree for 'which protocol should I use?'
description: Innovation idea #8 (3/3 gates). Interactive flowchart: 'What kind of communication? One-way → SSE. Two-way → WebSocket/WebRTC. Unreliable OK? → UDP. Need ordering? → TCP.' Addresses the #1 system design interview question.
acceptanceCriteria: Interactive flowchart with clickable decision nodes
Each terminal node links to the corresponding protocol simulation
Covers at least 6 protocols in the decision tree
Works as a standalone view accessible from sidebar

title: Add Protocol Diff view to compare any two protocols side by side
description: Innovation idea #3 — first-in-world feature. Select any 2 protocols and see differences highlighted header by header, handshake step by step, performance metric by metric. Like a git diff for protocols.
acceptanceCriteria: Dropdown to select 2 protocols for comparison
Side-by-side view with highlighted differences
Covers handshake steps, header fields, and performance metrics
Animated transitions between compared protocols

title: Add DHCP DORA handshake simulation
description: 4-step Discover-Offer-Request-Acknowledge handshake is a perfect fit for the existing sequence diagram component. Fills the Layer 3 gap. University curricula require it.
acceptanceCriteria: DHCP engine produces 4+ events for the DORA flow
Sequence diagram shows Client, DHCP Server, and broadcast messages
Step descriptions explain each phase and IP assignment

title: Add TLS attack simulation (MITM, SSL stripping, certificate forgery)
description: Innovation idea #13 — emotional 'gasp' moment. Watch a simulated MITM attack in real time. See the attacker intercept, forge certificate. Then toggle on certificate pinning and watch the attack FAIL. Visceral understanding of WHY TLS matters.
acceptanceCriteria: At least 2 attack scenarios (MITM, SSL stripping) with step-by-step visualization
Toggle to enable/disable protection and see different outcomes
Descriptions explain which handshake step is compromised
Red visual indicators for attack actions

title: Add Quick Cards view — one-screen protocol summary (Julia Evans style)
description: Innovation idea #6 (3/3 gates). One screen per protocol with key visual, essential facts, common gotchas. Inspired by Julia Evans' networking zines. Useful for cramming and quick reference.
acceptanceCriteria: Each protocol has a Quick Card accessible from sidebar or toggle
Card fits in a single viewport with no scrolling
Contains: visual diagram, 3-5 key facts, common mistakes, use cases
Shareable/exportable as image

title: Add 100-second autoplay overview mode per protocol
description: Innovation idea #5 (3/3 gates). Ultra-fast animated overview — no interaction needed, just watch and learn. Addresses 12-year-old, ADHD, and 2am student personas. Auto-advances with timed pauses on key moments.
acceptanceCriteria: Each protocol has a '100s Overview' button
Animation auto-advances through all steps with appropriate timing
Key moments pause briefly with highlighted annotation
Can be interrupted by clicking Step or Pause

title: Add Full OSI encapsulation visualizer (type URL, watch packet build layer by layer)
description: Innovation idea #35 — first-in-world potential. Type a URL. Watch data get encapsulated: Application → Transport → Network → Data Link → Physical. At each layer see the header added, size grow. Then watch decapsulation at server. The single most-asked concept in networking education.
acceptanceCriteria: URL input triggers encapsulation animation through all layers
Each layer clearly shows header being prepended with size
Reverse path shows decapsulation
Layer names, protocols, and header sizes are accurate

title: Add 'Break the Protocol' challenge mode
description: Innovation idea #20. Given a working protocol, user tries to break it: drop a packet, forge a response, replay a message. System shows what breaks and why the real protocol has that protection. Learn-by-breaking is more engaging than learn-by-watching.
acceptanceCriteria: At least 3 protocols support break-the-protocol challenges
User can inject faults: drop, reorder, forge, replay
System shows the consequences of each fault
Comparison with how the real protocol prevents it

title: Add export simulation as GIF/video for content creators
description: Innovation idea #17. Every exported GIF is free marketing. Use MediaRecorder API or html2canvas to capture simulation playback as an animated GIF or MP4. Addresses content creator and professor personas.
acceptanceCriteria: Export button appears on every protocol simulation
Creates animated GIF or MP4 of the simulation playback
Includes Architex watermark/attribution
Works in Chrome and Firefox
